extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe attacked solo on the steep Alto de Arriaz wall (2.4km at 20%) with 8km remaining, held off chasers Aranburu and Barcelo by 5 seconds after a tense descent into Bilbao.
tactical_patterns: Break formation delayed until 60km to go after 100km of attacks due to rest day creating excessive fresh legs.
Gilbert's double acceleration on steep gradients (20%) cracked Barcelo when single surge couldn't, exposing punchy climber vulnerability to repeated efforts.
Jumbo Visma deployed Bennett alone in finale while rivals had multiple riders, revealing GC confidence with minimal domestique support.

rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: At 37, out-of-saddle sprinting on 20% grades then holding off chasers on descent shows exceptional late-season form pre-Worlds
rider_signals.LASTRA Jonathan: positive: Attacked hard day after 2nd place finish, demonstrating recovery ability that most breakaway specialists lack
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: positive: Bridged 4-minute gap on climbs to catch leaders despite being known pure domestique, showing hidden climbing capacity
rider_signals.BENNETT George: neutral: Last remaining Roglic domestique on final climb among 20 riders suggests either tactical choice or team weakness
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: negative: Already dropped on first third-category climb despite fresh legs from rest day, signaling poor climbing form
